rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers used in AGREE contracts are visible in the component scope or properly imported.
Resolve type errors by aligning expression types with declared variable types and avoiding <error> types in binary operations.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always ensure that referenced identifiers are declared or visible in the same annex scope.
Maintain type consistency in expressions: numeric, boolean, and package-qualified references must match declared types.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers unless explicitly imported and available in the model.
When diagnostics report '<error>' types, trace back to the first unresolved reference and correct it.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE const and eq declarations must use valid scalar types: int, real, bool.
Binary expressions in AGREE must have operands of matching types.
Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex for guarantees and assumptions.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the model or its dependencies.
Do not write guarantees or assumptions referencing identifiers that are not declared or visible in the component scope.
Do not attempt to resolve <error> types by adding architecture; instead, remove or fix the invalid clause.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
